mnemonics: ROSE & SMOTE Oversample, Undersample the Rest
keyPoints: Accuracy is a misleading metric for imbalanced data; use Precision, Recall, F1-Score, or AUC-ROC instead.
Resampling techniques are fundamental strategies, including oversampling the minority class (e.g., SMOTE) and undersampling the majority class.
Algorithm-level approaches like cost-sensitive learning assign a higher penalty for misclassifying the minority class to adjust the model's focus.
commonMistakes: Using accuracy as the sole performance metric, which will be deceptively high if the model simply predicts the majority class.
Applying resampling techniques (especially random oversampling) without proper cross-validation, leading to severe overfitting.
Treating class imbalance as the only problem, when issues like insufficient data, irrelevant features, or noisy data could be the root cause.
